relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Four winds are held back at earth's corners.
A sunrise messenger carries the seal of living Divine.
Harm is restrained until servants are sealed.
Israel is heard as a sealed symbolic fullness.
Judah, Reuben, and Gad are named.
Asher, Naphtali, and Manasseh are named.
Simeon, Levi, and Issachar are named.
Zebulun, Joseph, and Benjamin complete the sealed number.
An innumerable multitude from every people stands before throne and Lamb.
They cry that salvation belongs to Divine and Lamb.
Blessing, glory, wisdom, thanks, honor, power, and strength return to Divine.
The elder asks who the white-robed ones are.
They come from great pressure, whitened in Lamb-life.
They serve before the throne and are sheltered.
Hunger, thirst, and scorching are ended.
The Lamb shepherds them to life-waters, and every tear is wiped away.

contemplative_note: Revelation 7 answers who can stand: sealed Israel, innumerable nations, sheltered worshipers, the Lamb as shepherd, and tears wiped away.
